guests.name: Thich Nhat Hanh
guests.bio: Thich Nhat Hanh (1926–2022) was a Vietnamese Zen master, poet, and peace activist whose accessible teachings on mindfulness and engaged Buddhism have influenced millions of practitioners worldwide.

guests.name: Pema Chödrön
guests.bio: Pema Chödrön is an American Buddhist nun and author in the Shambhala tradition, known for her teachings on working with fear, uncertainty, and compassion in everyday life.

guests.name: Rupert Spira
guests.bio: Rupert Spira is a contemporary teacher of non-duality whose work explores the direct experience of awareness and the nature of consciousness.

guests.name: Sharon Salzberg
guests.bio: Sharon Salzberg is a meditation teacher and New York Times bestselling author, renowned for her teachings on lovingkindness, mindfulness, and the integration of Buddhist practice into daily life.

guests.name: Tenzin Gyatso, 14th Dalai Lama
guests.bio: His Holiness the 14th Dalai Lama is the spiritual leader of Tibetan Buddhism, widely respected for his teachings on compassion, interdependence, and secular ethics.

topics.name: Daily Reflection
topics.description: Short contemplations and prompts designed for a brief daily spiritual check-in.

topics.name: Meditation
topics.description: Guided practices and discussions on meditation techniques from various traditions.

topics.name: Non-duality
topics.description: Teachings on the nature of consciousness, awareness, and the dissolution of subject–object separation.

topics.name: Ethics
topics.description: Explorations of moral philosophy, right action, and compassionate living in a complex world.

topics.name: Mindfulness
topics.description: Applied mindfulness in work, relationships, and everyday activities, grounded in contemplative traditions.

topics.name: Karma
topics.description: Classical and modern perspectives on cause and effect, responsibility, and the shaping of experience.

topics.name: Stoicism
topics.description: Practical wisdom from the Stoic philosophers on resilience, virtue, and inner freedom.

topics.name: Buddhism
topics.description: Core Buddhist teachings on the Four Noble Truths, compassion, and the path of awakening.

topics.name: Compassion
topics.description: Cultivating compassion and lovingkindness for oneself and others across different traditions.

topics.name: Zen
topics.description: Koans, zazen, and everyday Zen practice in contemporary life.

topics.name: Advaita Vedanta
topics.description: Non-dual teachings from the Advaita Vedanta tradition, exploring self-inquiry and liberation.

topics.name: Yoga Philosophy
topics.description: The philosophical roots of yoga, including the Yoga Sutras and Bhagavad Gita.

topics.name: Contemplative Science
topics.description: Research at the intersection of neuroscience, psychology, and contemplative practice.

topics.name: Modern Spirituality
topics.description: Integrating ancient wisdom with contemporary life, technology, and culture.

topics.name: Relationships
topics.description: Bringing mindfulness, compassion, and clear communication into relationships and community.

topics.name: Death and Dying
topics.description: Contemplations on mortality, grief, and the spiritual dimensions of dying.

hosts.name: Amara Lee
hosts.bio: Amara Lee is a contemplative teacher who blends early Buddhist mindfulness with modern psychology, offering gentle, practical guidance for weaving awareness into everyday life.

hosts.name: Marco Silva
hosts.bio: Marco Silva is a non-duality facilitator whose dialogues invite listeners to explore consciousness, presence, and the end of separation through direct experience.

hosts.name: Sophia Klein
hosts.bio: Philosopher and ethicist Sophia Klein explores how contemplative wisdom traditions illuminate questions of justice, responsibility, and flourishing in contemporary society.

hosts.name: David Nguyen
hosts.bio: Drawing on Stoicism and Buddhist thought, David Nguyen offers down-to-earth conversations about resilience, clarity, and cultivating inner freedom amidst daily challenges.

hosts.name: Li Mei Chen
hosts.bio: Li Mei Chen is a meditation teacher steeped in Zen and Tibetan traditions, offering guided practices and teachings that emphasize gentleness, curiosity, and compassion.

hosts.name: Ravi Iyer
hosts.bio: Ravi Iyer weaves together Advaita Vedanta, Yoga philosophy, and everyday ethics, inviting listeners into a rigorous yet warm-hearted path of inquiry.

hosts.name: Helen Fischer
hosts.bio: Neuroscientist and long-time meditator Helen Fischer explores the emerging science of contemplative practice and its implications for well-being and ethics.

hosts.name: Yuki Tanaka
hosts.bio: Zen priest Yuki Tanaka shares stories, koans, and reflections on bringing zazen, simplicity, and awareness to work, family, and the realities of loss.

hosts.name: Fatima Hassan
hosts.bio: With a background in counseling and Sufi practice, Fatima Hassan explores how presence, kindness, and clear communication transform our closest relationships.

hosts.name: Nora Álvarez
hosts.bio: Nora Álvarez offers brief, poetic daily reflections that weave together Christian mysticism, Buddhism, and secular mindfulness.

hosts.name: Thich Nhat Hanh (Archive Series)
hosts.bio: An archival collection of talks and interviews from Zen master Thich Nhat Hanh, focusing on engaged Buddhism, mindful living, and peace.

hosts.name: Rupert Spira (Conversations)
hosts.bio: A series of in-depth conversations with Rupert Spira on the direct path of non-duality, awareness, and the end of suffering.

series.title: Stoicism
series.description: A practical series exploring Stoic philosophy through modern scenarios, from workplace stress to personal loss, with guided reflections and exercises.

series.title: Buddhism
series.description: Foundational teachings on the Four Noble Truths, the Eightfold Path, and compassion, presented through talks, dialogues, and short practices.

series.title: Daily Reflection
series.description: Brief contemplations for each day of the week, offering a question or image to carry into your day in under ten minutes.

series.title: Non-duality Dialogues
series.description: Long-form conversations with contemplative teachers on awareness, the sense of self, and the direct path beyond separation.

series.title: Mindfulness in Everyday Life
series.description: Applied mindfulness for commuting, caregiving, conflict, and creativity, with guided practices woven into real-world stories.

series.title: Ethics for a Complex World
series.description: Philosophical and contemplative perspectives on difficult moral questions, from technology to climate and social justice.

series.title: Karma and Cause & Effect
series.description: Stories, teachings, and Q&A episodes unpacking traditional and modern interpretations of karma and responsibility.

series.title: Thich Nhat Hanh Teachings
series.description: An archival collection of talks from Thich Nhat Hanh on mindful living, interbeing, and engaged Buddhism, restored for modern listeners.

series.title: Rupert Spira on Awareness
series.description: A curated run of dialogues with Rupert Spira focused on the felt sense of awareness, suffering, and the discovery of peace.

series.title: Science of Meditation
series.description: Researchers and practitioners discuss what brain and behavioral science are learning from long-term contemplative practice.

series.title: Death, Dying, and Awakening
series.description: Gentle conversations about mortality, grief, and the possibility of awakening in the face of impermanence.

episodes.title: Daily Reflection: Breathing with the Morning Light
episodes.subtitle: A 10-minute pause to arrive in the new day
episodes.description: Nora Álvarez offers a brief contemplation on the first breath of the morning, inviting you to notice light, body, and intention before the day’s demands begin.

episodes.title: Daily Reflection: Listening Between Thoughts
episodes.subtitle: Notice the quiet spaces in your inner dialogue
episodes.description: A short audio postcard inviting you to listen for the silence between thoughts and discover the ease already present in your morning.

episodes.title: Daily Reflection: A Question for Today
episodes.subtitle: One guiding question to carry into your day
episodes.description: Nora offers a single, open-ended question to hold lightly as you move through ordinary tasks, inviting curiosity rather than self-judgment.

episodes.title: Daily Reflection: Touching the Ground
episodes.subtitle: Feeling the support beneath your feet
episodes.description: This reflection invites you to feel into the literal and emotional ground that supports you, even amid uncertainty.

episodes.title: Daily Reflection: A Bow to Imperfection
episodes.subtitle: Softening around mistakes and missteps
episodes.description: Nora reflects on how spiritual practice includes our imperfections, inviting a bow of respect toward the parts of us that struggle.

episodes.title: Mindfulness Morning Practice: Gentle Body Scan
episodes.subtitle: A 25-minute grounding before your day begins
episodes.description: Amara Lee guides a spacious, gentle body scan designed to be your morning anchor, helping you greet the day with embodied presence rather than autopilot.

episodes.title: Mindfulness Before Email: 30 Breaths of Steadiness
episodes.subtitle: Pause and center before opening your inbox
episodes.description: David Nguyen offers a simple 32-minute practice: thirty breaths of mindful attention and reflection on intention before engaging with digital life.

episodes.title: Mindfulness on a Lunch-Break Walk
episodes.subtitle: A 38-minute guided urban walking meditation
episodes.description: Join Amara Lee for a mindful walk through city streets, learning to meet noise, distraction, and beauty with the same curious awareness.

episodes.title: Non-duality and Everyday Awareness (with Rupert Spira)
episodes.subtitle: Recognizing awareness in the midst of ordinary life
episodes.description: Marco Silva and Rupert Spira explore how the felt sense of awareness is always present, even during conflict, boredom, and ordinary chores, with time for guided self-inquiry.

episodes.title: Embracing Groundlessness (with Pema Chödrön)
episodes.subtitle: Letting go of the search for solid ground
episodes.description: In this dialogue, Marco Silva and Pema Chödrön explore how non-dual insight meets the messy realities of fear, loss, and uncertainty, with practical pointers for staying present.

episodes.title: Who Are We, Really? A Non-dual Panel Conversation
episodes.subtitle: A multi-teacher exploration of self and awareness
episodes.description: Marco Silva moderates a conversation with Rupert Spira and Sharon Salzberg on the sense of self, relational practice, and the meeting point between non-duality and lovingkindness.

episodes.title: Stoic Tools for Uncertain Times
episodes.subtitle: Practicing courage and clarity when the future is unclear
episodes.description: David Nguyen unpacks core Stoic practices—negative visualization, the view from above, and focusing on what’s in our control—and pairs them with short reflections you can return to throughout the week.

episodes.title: Stoicism and Letting Go of Control
episodes.subtitle: Releasing what we cannot shape
episodes.description: In the latest installment of the Stoicism series, David Nguyen explores the Stoic teaching of amor fati—loving one’s fate—and how it can soften anxiety without leading to passivity.

episodes.title: The Four Foundations of Mindfulness
episodes.subtitle: Body, feeling tone, mind, and dharmas in daily life
episodes.description: Li Mei Chen walks through the classical four foundations of mindfulness, offering practical, accessible ways to explore each foundation during an ordinary day.

episodes.title: Compassion in Action
episodes.subtitle: From meditation cushion to daily interactions
episodes.description: In this episode, Li Mei Chen explores how the cultivation of compassion moves beyond feeling into concrete actions, including clear boundaries and social engagement.

episodes.title: Thich Nhat Hanh: The Bell of Mindfulness
episodes.subtitle: Returning to yourself with each sound of the bell
episodes.description: An archival talk from Thich Nhat Hanh on how a simple bell can call us back to ourselves, transforming moments of stress into moments of presence.

episodes.title: Thich Nhat Hanh: Walking Meditation
episodes.subtitle: Each step as a homecoming
episodes.description: Thich Nhat Hanh guides a walking meditation, inviting you to let each step be a gentle arrival in the present moment, with suggestions for practicing in everyday places.

episodes.title: Karma and Intention: How Actions Shape Experience
episodes.subtitle: A practical look at cause, effect, and responsibility
episodes.description: Sophia Klein and Ravi Iyer, joined by the Dalai Lama in an archival clip, explore classic teachings on karma, clarifying common misconceptions and offering reflections for everyday ethical choices.
